item.title: SpaceX nears lift-off
item.description: An IPO so big it could change the Nasdaq

item.title: Iran starts to restore internet access after months-long blackout
item.description: Move to allow connectivity comes as US strikes Iranian missile launch sites and peace talks continue

item.title: Brussels set to open in-depth probe into JD.com’s takeover of Ceconomy
item.description: Decision would be first time EU conducted detailed review of Chinese takeover

item.title: Chipmaker ETF rides AI excitement to quickest $10bn valuation on record
item.description: Roundhill Memory ETF, known as DRAM, surges 87% within 50 days of April launch

item.title: BP is deeply dysfunctional, but it could be worse
item.description: Investors should be concerned by Manifold’s ousting, but better to fire too many executives than too few

item.title: UK law firm Pinsent Masons reprimanded by court over AI error
item.description: Judge Mark Mullen warns lawyers against outsourcing legal research or reasoning

item.title: Ferrari’s first EV sparks investor and social media backlash
item.description: Luce launch comes as executives are divided over demand for luxury electric cars

item.title: AI tools lead to ‘clear racial disparities’ in job hiring
item.description: New Stanford-led study finds candidates that fail AI-hiring tests face ‘systemic rejection’ across companies

item.title: Philanthropists launch $140mn push for Strep A vaccine
item.description: Bacterium causes similar numbers of deaths to malaria and HIV/Aids, but attracts far less funding

item.title: BP removes chair Albert Manifold over ‘serious concerns’ about his conduct
item.description: Manifold’s hands-on approach was viewed as excessive by several colleagues at oil major

item.title: Today’s windfall is a warning for state oil companies
item.description: Continuing to invest as if yesterday’s assumptions will govern tomorrow’s world means they won’t be around in 40 years

item.title: Hedge funds are AImaxxing
item.description: Software is out, semis are in

item.title: ECB says private-credit fuelled AI boom poses risk to financial system
item.description: Central bank warns that investors could be exposed to losses if the technology does not meet its potential

item.title: Mango heir Jonathan Andic stands down over probe into father’s fatal fall
item.description: Fashion chain’s vice-chair says investigation into death of company’s billionaire founder requires his full attention

item.title: Ford’s convenient comp comp
item.description: Peer factor

item.title: US car dealers face backlash over practices as prices surge
item.description: Pressure mounts on traditional sales model as regulators increase scrutiny and competitors offer new approach

item.title: Fewer households should be exempt from energy bills, says regulator
item.description: British customers owe a record £4.5bn to their power suppliers, 71 per cent more than in 2023, according to Ofgem

item.title: More takeaways from an S-1 for the ages
item.description: Extending our consciousness, one risk factor at a time

item.title: ByteDance offers AI team special stock to fend off poaching
item.description: TikTok owner issues shares tied to AI business unit as China’s tech talent war heats up

item.title: Ferrari launches first EV with Jony Ive’s ‘polarising’ design
item.description: Shares in carmaker fall as futuristic Luce model sparks negative reaction on social media

item.title: FTAV’s further reading
item.description: Is AI profitable yet; Enhanced Games; SpaceX; Shoreditch lunching

item.title: FirstFT: US strikes Iranian missile sites
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: shrinking real wages and bank deregulation

item.title: AI and the brave new world of deals
item.description: Global M&A is now dominated by the race to control the world’s energy, fibre networks and compute

item.title: SpaceX to drive a cybertruck through corporate governance norms
item.description: Provisions in the IPO prospectus will give Elon Musk more freedom — and potentially less accountability

item.title: AI stories aren’t inevitably ‘not art’
item.description: Exercising our own judgment when it comes to quality is something we should not outsource to machines

item.title: Spotify chief defends AI-generated music
item.description: Streaming app strikes deal with Universal allowing subscribers to create ‘controlled’ covers and remixes

item.title: Major European airlines exposed to €1.5bn-plus in carbon costs, analysis shows
item.description: Extension of emissions trading scheme opposed by flag carriers

item.title: Monzo spends heavily on ‘refer a friend’ rewards to boost market share
item.description: London-based fintech records ‘staggering’ increase in spending on referral fees

item.title: Deregulation hands top US and UK banks $1.3tn opening
item.description: Regulatory loosening has allowed countries’ banks to expand balance sheets while EU and Swiss rivals are constrained

item.title: Top European wind turbine maker calls ‘non-western’ rivals a security threat
item.description: Nordex CEO José Luis Blanco says region’s ‘supply chain independence’ must be protected
